fixture.purpose: Expose all four switch terminals independently so assembly fit, internal common groups, released isolation, actuated closure, contact resistance, and bounce can be measured without assuming topology in the coupon copper.

design_verification.copper_rule: No copper connection exists between any two DUT terminals.

planned_assembly.process: Stencil print and infrared reflow using the manufacturer page 2 profile, followed by optical inspection.
required_record: black solder mask, white silkscreen, and ENIG finish confirmation
top and side inspection images for every specimen

planned_functional_test.fixture: pts815-coupon-rev-a with four independent 3 mm terminal pads suitable for paired probes or Kelvin clips
instruments: four-wire resistance meter or source-measure unit with fixture compensation
insulation resistance meter capable of resolving at least 100 Mohm
500 ohm 1 percent load for approximately 10 mA
oscilloscope with at least 1 MS/s acquisition
sequence: Photograph and inspect pad alignment, seating, solder wetting, bridges, actuator access, and body clearance.
With the actuator released, measure resistance from terminal 1 to 2 and from terminal 3 to 4.
With the actuator released, measure isolation between every terminal in group 1-2 and every terminal in group 3-4.
At 5 VDC and approximately 10 mA, perform 100 full press-release cycles while recording contact state and bounce duration.
After cycling, repeat common-pair resistance, released isolation, actuated resistance, seating, and solder-joint inspection.
acceptance: Released terminal 1-2 and terminal 3-4 resistance each meet the datasheet 100 mohm initial limit after declared fixture compensation and uncertainty.
Released cross-group insulation is at least 100 Mohm.
Every commanded actuation closes between the two groups and every release opens between them.
No recorded bounce interval exceeds 10 ms.
No solder bridge, lifted terminal, body interference, permanent preload, intermittent connection, or visible damage is observed.

not_proven_by_this_plan: wash, coating, moisture, ingress, vibration, shock, or corrosive-environment suitability
automotive, medical, safety, or other regulated qualification
production yield across multiple PCB, stencil, paste, assembly, and component lots
